remediation_steps.next_best_action: Investigate Outlook crash on large inline-content emails. Check for known issues with Microsoft 365 Apps Version 2403 Build 17425.20228 handling large HTML emails with embedded images. Consider Outlook memory limits and the March Windows update as potential factors.
remediation_steps: Check for known issues with Outlook Version 2403 and large HTML emails with embedded images
Review Event Viewer crash data for OUTLOOK.EXE exception code 0xc0000005 to identify the faulting module
Test with a freshly created Outlook profile and a copy of one of the problematic emails
If the issue is related to the March Windows update, check for a cumulative update fix or roll back the update
Consider configuring Outlook to download only email headers for the automated report folder as a workaround
Rebuild the Outlook search index to fix the search issue caused by crash-related corruption

remediation_steps.next_best_action: Investigate persistent packet loss on London office Floor 2 LAN. Access switch replacement did not resolve the issue — traceroute data shows losses at hop 3 (core switch 10.44.0.254). Focus investigation on the core switch rather than the access layer.
remediation_steps: Investigate the core switch at 10.44.0.254 for packet loss — review interface error counters, CPU utilization, and spanning-tree topology
Check for duplex mismatches or failing transceivers on the uplink from Floor 2 access switch to the core
Review recent network changes or firmware updates that coincide with the March 11 start date
If core switch is faulty, plan failover to redundant path and schedule replacement
Escalate to vendor support if the core switch diagnostics are inconclusive

remediation_steps.next_best_action: Investigate data corruption in legacy CRM to Dynamics 365 migration export. Approximately 30% of 12,000 records have encoding corruption (null bytes, mixed collation, UTF-16 fragments). The legacy SQL Server 2008 source has mixed collation settings contributing to the issue.
remediation_steps: Analyze the source SQL Server 2008 database collation settings across all relevant tables
Build a data cleansing pipeline that handles encoding normalization, null byte removal, and UTF-16 to UTF-8 conversion
Re-export the corrupted records directly from the source database with explicit encoding settings
Validate each record against the Dynamics 365 schema before import
Run a reconciliation report comparing source and destination record counts and data integrity
Document the encoding issues and migration process for the decommissioning audit trail

Increase the memory limit for the report-service container from 4GB to an appropriate higher value

Add memory usage monitoring and alerts to catch similar issues before OOM kills
